default.speaker: Mara
default.text: Quiet today. But then, it usually is.

vairagya.speaker: Mara
vairagya.text: You know, I used to count the people who came in. Kept a little notebook.

vairagya.speaker: Mara
vairagya.text: Stopped a few years back. Not sure why.

vairagya.speaker: Mara
vairagya.text: Maybe counting things doesn't make them more real. Just more... counted.

nihilism.speaker: Mara
nihilism.text: You want the menu? It hasn't changed in four years.

nihilism.speaker: Mara
nihilism.text: I print new ones sometimes. Same items. Different paper.

nihilism.speaker: Mara
nihilism.text: The council sent someone about the hygiene rating. Said I should 'refresh the branding.'

nihilism.speaker: Mara
nihilism.text: I asked him what that meant. He couldn't say. Not really.

absurdism.speaker: Mara
absurdism.text: I burned the first pot today. Completely forgot it was on.

absurdism.speaker: Mara
absurdism.text: Stood there watching it smoke and thought: well. That's a kind of cooking.

absurdism.speaker: Mara
absurdism.text: Made a fresh one. Tastes better when the first one fails.

existentialism.speaker: Mara
existentialism.text: ...I almost didn't open today. Woke up and thought: why.

existentialism.speaker: Mara
existentialism.text: Then I thought: because someone might need it. Might need the door to be open.

existentialism.speaker: Mara
existentialism.text: And if it's closed — if I don't — then they walk past and the day is different.

existentialism.speaker: Mara
existentialism.text: So I opened it. Like always.
